data.title: Bond Investors Eye U.S. Jobs Report for Potential Impact on Interest Rates
data.description: Bond investors are closely monitoring the monthly U.S. jobs report, which could influence their expectations regarding Federal Reserve interest-rate cuts. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that since the onset of the Iran war, inflation has been a primary concern for these investors. The upcoming jobs data may present unexpected results, potentially altering the current outlook on monetary policy adjustments. Investors are particularly attentive to any surprises that could shift the trajectory of interest rates, impacting bond market dynamics.

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description: 1. ParaFi Capital is suspected of swapping AAVE for SKY, involving approximately $5.38 million; 2. Short-selling firm Culper Research: has shorted Ethereum and related securities, Fusaka upgrade damages token economic models; 3. SEC Chairman states: looks forward to cooperating with CFTC Chairman to advance the CLARITY Act; 4. Wallets associated with the Pump.fun team deposited 1.757 billion PUMP, worth $3.54 million, into Bitget; 5. Anthropic: its designation as a national security supply chain risk lacks sufficient legal basis; 6. Bessant: temporarily allows India to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea; 7. OpenAI: officially launches GPT-5.4; 8. An Ethereum ICO wallet, dormant for 10.6 years, has been activated, with an initial investment of $125 now worth $834,000, a return of approximately 6687 times; 9. UAE considers freezing billions of dollars in Iranian assets.

data.title: Philippines Considers Energy-Saving Measures Amid Rising Global Fuel Costs
data.description: The Philippines is exploring strategies to reduce energy consumption as escalating tensions in the Middle East drive global fuel prices upward. Bloomberg posted on X, the government has recommended that citizens limit the use of air conditioners and avoid non-essential travel to mitigate the impact of rising costs. These measures are part of a broader effort to manage energy expenses and ensure stability in the face of international market fluctuations. The government is actively seeking solutions to address the challenges posed by the volatile fuel market, aiming to balance economic needs with environmental considerations.

data.title: Japan Considers Easing Restrictions on Weapon Exports
data.description: Japan's ruling Liberal Democratic Party and the Japan Restoration Party have submitted a proposal to Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi advocating for the removal of five types of restrictions on weapon exports. According to Jin10, the proposal suggests allowing the transfer of weapons with lethal capabilities in principle. The Japanese government plans to review this proposal and may revise the relevant guidelines as early as this spring.

data.title: Anthropic Faces Potential Legal Battle Over AI Safeguards Dispute
data.description: Anthropic is facing a potential legal confrontation with the U.S. Defense Department over disagreements regarding AI safeguards. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the company is threatened with blacklisting due to the ongoing dispute. The conflict centers around the implementation and adequacy of safety measures in AI technologies, which the Defense Department deems insufficient. As tensions rise, both parties are preparing for a possible court case to resolve the matter. The outcome of this legal battle could have significant implications for the future of AI regulation and industry standards.

data.title: South Korean President Warns Oil Refiners Against Price Collusion
data.description: South Korean President Lee Jae-myung has issued a stern warning to oil refining companies, accusing them of potentially colluding to inflate oil prices. According to Jin10, President Lee emphasized that price collusion is a serious crime that harms public interest. He stated that those involved will soon realize the severe consequences of their actions.

data.title: STOCKS | Goldman Sachs Raises KOSPI Index Target Amid Strong Chip Prices
data.description: Goldman Sachs has increased its target for South Korea's KOSPI index from 6,500 points to 7,000 points, citing the continued strength in memory chip prices. According to Jin10, strategists including Timothy Moe suggest that the recent market pullback is a correction, and after a period of consolidation, the index is likely to rebound and reach new highs. Goldman Sachs has also raised its 2026 earnings forecast for South Korea from 120% to 130%, marking the third upward revision this year.

data.title: Dormant Ethereum ICO Wallet Moves 100.27 ETH After 10.6 Years
data.description: A dormant Ethereum ICO wallet, inactive for over a decade, has transferred 100.27 ETH to a new wallet address. According to Foresight News, the wallet initially acquired 401.1 ETH for just $125, which is now valued at approximately $834,000.

data.title: Strike Secures BitLicense and Money Transmitter License in New York
data.description: Bitcoin payments app Strike has successfully obtained both a BitLicense and a Money Transmitter License from the New York State Department of Financial Services. According to NS3.AI, these licenses enable Strike to provide Bitcoin-related products and services within New York. The BitLicense is known for being one of the most stringent regulatory licenses for digital asset businesses in the United States. Previously, Strike introduced a U.S. credit service that allows users to borrow cash against their Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: Culper Research, a short-selling firm, has shorted Ethereum and related securities; the Fusaka upgrade is damaging to token economic models.
data.description: Culper Research published a statement on the X platform stating that it has shorted Ethereum and related securities, including BMNR. Culper Research believes that Ethereum's token economic model was damaged following the Fusaka upgrade in December 2025. The firm claims that Ethereum founder Vitalik Buterin is aware of this problem and is selling off, while Ethereum supporter Tom Lee is "knowingly doing the wrong thing." Culper Research predicts that the price of ETH will fall further.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Market Declines, Hong Kong ADRs Drop
data.description: The U.S. stock market experienced a downturn, leading to a decrease in Hong Kong ADRs by 72 points or 0.28%, closing at 25,250 points. According to Ming Pao, the immediate month Hang Seng Index futures fell by 150 points, settling at 25,037 points, with a discount of 284 points in the night session.

data.title: Asian Currencies Show Strength Amid Technical Recovery
data.description:
Most Asian currencies have experienced a strengthening trend, attributed to a likely technical recovery. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that this development comes as investors anticipate a rebound following recent declines. The recovery is seen as a response to market dynamics rather than fundamental economic changes.

Analysts suggest that the strengthening of these currencies is part of a broader market correction. This trend is observed across several Asian economies, where currencies had previously weakened due to various external pressures.

Market participants are closely monitoring these movements, as they could signal shifts in investor sentiment and potential adjustments in monetary policies. The focus remains on how these currencies will perform in the coming weeks, given the ongoing global economic uncertainties.

Overall, the technical recovery of Asian currencies highlights the complex interplay of market forces and investor expectations in the region's financial landscape.

data.title: MiniMed Raises $560 Million in U.S. IPO Below Market Range
data.description: MiniMed, a company specializing in diabetes management, successfully raised $560 million through its initial public offering (IPO) in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X that the IPO was priced below the initially marketed range. MiniMed is set to be spun off from the health-care giant Medtronic, marking a significant step in its journey as an independent entity. The decision to price the IPO below the expected range reflects current market conditions and investor sentiment. This move is part of a broader strategy to establish MiniMed as a standalone company, focusing on innovative solutions for diabetes management. The funds raised will likely support the company's growth and development initiatives as it navigates the competitive landscape of the healthcare industry.

data.title: New Zealand's National Party Faces Decline in Support Ahead of Election
data.description: Support for New Zealand's ruling National Party has decreased according to a recent opinion poll. Bloomberg posted on X that Prime Minister Christopher Luxon is facing challenges in gaining voter confidence as the country approaches this year's election. The decline in support comes as the party navigates various political and economic issues, impacting its standing with the electorate. The upcoming election will be a critical test for Luxon's leadership and the party's ability to address voter concerns.

data.title: Banco Master Owner Transferred to Federal Prison in Brasília
data.description: Daniel Vorcaro, the owner of the defunct Banco Master, is set to be relocated under heightened security measures to a federal prison in Brasília. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increased security surrounding Vorcaro's transfer. The decision follows the bank's collapse, which has drawn significant attention from financial regulators and the public. Authorities are ensuring that the transfer process is conducted with utmost caution, reflecting the seriousness of the situation. Vorcaro's move to the federal facility marks a critical development in the ongoing investigation into the bank's failure.

data.title: Anthropic: There is insufficient legal basis to designate it as a national security supply chain risk.
data.description: On March 5, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei issued a statement saying that Anthropic received a formal letter from the U.S. Department of Defense (now known as the War Department) on March 4, confirming that the company had been identified as a national security supply chain risk. Amodei stated that this designation lacks sufficient legal basis and the company will challenge it through legal channels. Amodei pointed out that the wording of the Department of Defense letter indicates that the designation only applies to situations where customers directly use Claude in Department of Defense contracts and does not affect Claude usage scenarios unrelated to Department of Defense contracts. He reiterated that Anthropic's negotiations with the Department of Defense have been ongoing, and both sides have communicated on service methods and transition plans. Anthropic maintains two bottom lines: opposition to large-scale domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons. Amodei apologized for a previously leaked internal company post, stating that the post was written on the same day that Trump announced the removal of Anthropic, Secretary of Defense Hegseth issued the supply chain risk designation, and OpenAI reached an agreement with the Pentagon, and that the tone was not fully considered. He stated that Anthropic's top priority is ensuring that frontline warfighters and national security experts do not lose critical tools during major operations, and the company will continue to provide model services to the Department of Defense and national security agencies at a nominal fee.

data.title: A wallet associated with the Pump.fun team deposited 1.757 billion Pumps, worth $3.54 million, into Bitget.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet associated with the Pump.fun team deposited 1.757 billion PUMPs, worth $3.54 million, into Bitget. The wallet currently holds 12.3 billion PUMPs, worth $24.77 million.

data.title: Anthropic Opposes Autonomous Weapons and Domestic Surveillance
data.description: Anthropic has reiterated its stance against fully autonomous weapons and extensive domestic surveillance. According to Jin10, the company plans to offer its models to the U.S. Department of Defense and the national security sector at a symbolic cost. Additionally, Anthropic's engineers will provide ongoing technical support to these entities. This move underscores the company's commitment to ethical considerations in the deployment of AI technologies.

data.title: Shenzhen Stock Exchange Updates Hong Kong Stock Connect Securities List
data.description: The Shenzhen Stock Exchange has announced updates to the Hong Kong Stock Connect securities list. According to Jin10, Muyuan Foods and Han's CNC have been added to the list of eligible securities. These changes will take effect on March 6. The inclusion of these companies is part of ongoing efforts to enhance the connectivity between the mainland and Hong Kong financial markets, providing investors with more diverse investment opportunities.

data.title: On Polymarket, the probability of OKX's IPO in 2026 is currently estimated at 17%.
data.description: Data from Polymarket shows that the probability of OKX's IPO in 2026 has fallen from a high of 57% in early January to a current 17%, with trading volume exceeding $400,000. Previously, it was reported that ICE, the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange, invested in OKX at a valuation of $25 billion. ICE declined to disclose the specific investment amount or terms, but emphasized the shared vision between the two companies.
